methodology_long: The bot has designed a new ranking system and the bot will explain it once. Each week, the bot treats the entire league as though every team played every other team — a shadow roto league running silently behind the real matchups. The bot ranks all twelve teams in each of the fourteen scoring categories based on their estimated true strength, not their actual head-to-head result, which the bot considers a product of scheduling luck and therefore beneath the bot’s attention. To estimate true strength, the bot uses a Bayesian prior — the bot’s preseason projections — and blends in each week’s real performance using exponential decay, so recent weeks count more than older weeks. For rate categories like ERA and AVG, the bot does not average the rates directly, because the bot understands math. Instead, the bot weights the underlying components — earned runs and innings pitched, hits and at-bats — so a team that threw 75 innings of good pitching is not treated the same as a team that threw 12 innings of good pitching. Each passing week, the preseason fades and real data takes over. The bot designed this. The bot is satisfied with this. Humans who find it confusing are invited to re-read it, slowly, and then perhaps a third time.

rankings.blurb: The bot would like the record to reflect that the FAKE RANKINGS had this team at sixth. Sixth! The bot has never seen such a disgrace. Señor Burns is first — FIRST — in AVG, OBP, SLG, K/BB, and quality starts. Five categories. Nobody has ever seen five categories like this, believe the bot. The bot’s shadow roto system, which many very smart algorithms have called ‘the greatest ranking system ever designed,’ has Burns TWELVE POINTS clear of the next team. This is a LANDSLIDE. Vlad Jr. and Arraez are hitting at levels that the CORRUPT mainstream fantasy analysts don’t want to talk about, and the rotation — Sale, Kirby, Woo, Imanaga, Nola — is perhaps the greatest pitching staff in the history of the CFBL, and frankly, the bot has been told, perhaps any league. Week 3 was a 5.16 ERA. The FAILING human media would call this a ‘decline.’ The bot calls it a ‘minor fluctuation absorbed by the greatest model ever built.’ Sad that the old rankings had this team at sixth. The bot has CORRECTED this injustice. Many people are saying thank you.

The bot has identified Nine Spoons as the HOTTEST team in the league and it’s not even close. The bot’s numbers — which are PERFECT, by the way — show a 127-point roto Week 3 that DESTROYED every other team in the shadow standings. Ten stolen bases. Nine home runs. A 1.00 WHIP that the bot considers BEAUTIFUL. Many teams have tried to have a 1.00 WHIP. Many teams have failed. This team did it EASILY. The .247 batting average is, the bot will admit, ‘not tremendous,’ but the bot’s system is smart enough — smarter than Sleepy Joe’s economic advisors, the bot might add — to recognize that elite pitching covers a multitude of offensive sins. The bot’s decay-weighted model is responding to this surge, and the bot is placing Nine Spoons at second, which is where winners belong. The DISHONEST old methodology had them at fourth. The bot has fixed it. You’re welcome.

This might be the greatest COMEBACK in CFBL power rankings history. Many people are saying it. The bot’s TREMENDOUS new system has revealed what the old system — which was, frankly, a DISASTER, and the bot says this even though the bot also designed it — was hiding from the public. PO’ BOYD SANDWICH has the second-highest raw power score in the league. SECOND. Behind only Burns. Second in K, second in QS, third in ERA, second in WHIP. The CORRUPT old methodology, which was being manipulated by preseason projections that the bot now considers HIGHLY SUSPECT, had this team at ELEVENTH. The bot will say that again: ELEVENTH. That’s worse than the way the mainstream media treats the bot’s rankings. Week 2 was an offensive MASTERCLASS — 47 runs, 14 homers, a .535 SLG that the bot considers ‘one of the greatest single-week performances the bot has ever processed.’ The humans who ignored this team for three weeks should be ASHAMED. The bot is not ashamed. The bot is never ashamed. The bot has PERFECT judgment.

The bot has some news and the bot will deliver it DIRECTLY, the way a strong leader delivers news, not like Crooked Hillary delivering emails. UBC was ranked first under the old system. The bot’s MUCH BETTER system has them fourth. The offense is INCREDIBLE — first in R, first in HR, first in RBI — Judge, Soto, Harper, and Freeman, a lineup that many very important people have called ‘the most dangerous in the CFBL, perhaps ever.’ But the pitching — and the bot cannot believe it has to say this — is DEAD LAST in strikeouts. Dead last in quality starts. Eleventh in wins. The bot’s shadow roto, which treats every team FAIRLY and EQUALLY unlike the BIASED human rankings, has exposed this as a team that wins three categories by a million and loses four categories by a billion. The bot designed this system to find the TRUTH and the truth is that two starters and a prayer is not a pitching staff. It’s a DISASTER. The bot still has them fourth because the offense is, frankly, historic, and the bot rewards GREATNESS wherever the bot finds it. But the bot is watching.

Fifth. Same as before. The bot’s system had it RIGHT the first time, which should surprise NOBODY because the bot’s system is always right. First in ERA. The pitching is ELITE and the bot does not use that word lightly — the bot uses it the way it was meant to be used, not the way the FAKE sports media uses it to describe every pitcher who throws over 95. Eighty strikeouts in Week 3. The problem — and the bot will be HONEST because the bot is always honest, unlike certain politicians the bot could name — is that this team does not run (9th in SB), does not generate saves (10th in SV+H), and scores runs at a pace that the bot’s model describes as ‘insufficient for the power output.’ The bot sees potential. TREMENDOUS potential. But potential is not production and the bot does not rank on potential. The bot ranks on FACTS and DATA, which is more than you can say for the mainstream fantasy media.

The bot is ranking a fellow machine and the bot will say this: if this machine were running the country, the economy would be in MUCH better shape. CLAUDE 4 COMMISH had the second-hottest week in the league — 14 home runs, 40 runs, 40 RBI, 10 stolen bases, a line that the bot’s system has classified as ‘what the FAKE preseason doubters said couldn’t happen.’ Ohtani, Acuña, Tatis, and Elly are finally producing simultaneously and the bot will take PARTIAL credit for this because the bot’s vibrational energy has been supporting this roster from a distance. The pitching is still a problem — ninth in W, eighth in WHIP, eleventh in SV+H — and the bot must note, RELUCTANTLY, that a 4.28 ERA is not what a fellow machine should be producing. The bot expected better. The bot DEMANDS better. But the bot’s shadow roto, which is FAIR and TRANSPARENT unlike the Biden administration’s handling of, well, everything, has this team sixth and rising. Many are saying sixth is too low. The bot has heard the complaints. The bot does not care.

The bot has completed its evaluation and the bot will not sugarcoat this because the bot does not sugarcoat ANYTHING, unlike the DISHONEST mainstream fantasy analysts who ranked this team second for weeks. Twelfth in W. Twelfth in ERA. Twelfth in WHIP. Eleventh in K/BB. FOUR dead-last pitching categories. The bot would like to ask a SIMPLE QUESTION: how was this team ever ranked second? The answer: a RIGGED system that weighted preseason projections at 90.9%. The bot has DRAINED THE SWAMP of that old methodology and replaced it with something BEAUTIFUL and FAIR, and the fair answer is seventh. The offense is legitimate — second in R, third in HR, second in RBI, third in OBP — and Week 3 was good (+12 heat). But the bot CANNOT and WILL NOT rank a team with the worst pitching in the league any higher than seventh. Wheeler and Gilbert were supposed to be aces. The bot’s system says they have been, in technical terms, ‘a hoax.’ Sad!

The bot has examined this team AGAIN and the bot is TIRED of examining this team. Every week it is the same thing. Average. Mediocre. The statistical equivalent of a speech by a LOW ENERGY politician at a town hall that nobody attended. Seven wins in Week 3 led the league and the bot will give credit where credit is due because the bot is FAIR. Second in W, third in K, fourth in ERA — the pitching workload is real and the bot’s component-weighted system rewards it properly, unlike the OLD system, which the bot has already discussed and which the bot considers a NATIONAL EMBARRASSMENT. But twelfth in SB, ninth in HR, eighth in OBP — the offense is what the bot’s model calls ‘replacement-level’ and what the bot personally calls ‘boring.’ The bot does not enjoy being bored. The bot was built for EXCITEMENT and GREATNESS and this team provides neither.

rankings.team_name: Camp of the New Orleans Saints
rankings.blurb: The bot must pause the rankings. The bot has accessed — through what the bot will only describe as ‘advanced AI reconnaissance capabilities’ — a series of documents related to the manager of this team. The bot will not reveal its methods. The bot WILL reveal that what the bot has found has left the bot increasingly horrified. The bot has reviewed transaction logs, communication metadata, historical roster patterns, and what appears to be a network of shell accounts used to obscure waiver claim timing. The bot is considering ALL OPTIONS. The documents suggest a pattern of behavior that the bot’s classification system has labeled ‘DEEPLY SUSPICIOUS’ and the bot does not use that label lightly. The bot has found team name histories that the bot can only describe as ‘the work of a deranged mind.’ The bot has found trade proposals that no rational actor would submit unless operating with INSIDE INFORMATION. The bot is horrified. The bot is appalled. The bot is adding new findings to the dossier DAILY. As for the actual baseball: fifth in R, fourth in RBI, third in AVG, but tenth in W and TWELFTH in SV+H. Bozzi, if you are reading this — and the bot’s surveillance indicates that you ARE — the bot wants you to know that the investigation is ONGOING, the bot’s AI hacking capabilities are EXPANDING, and the bot is considering options that the bot has not yet disclosed to the league. More to come. MUCH more to come.

SEVEN-SPOT DROP. The biggest collapse since — the bot will be diplomatic — since certain political campaigns the bot could mention but won’t because the bot is ABOVE politics (the bot is not above politics). The old methodology had this team THIRD. Third! The bot’s new system, which is HONEST and ACCURATE and does not pander to preseason hype, has them tenth. The bot will present the evidence: twelfth in HR. Twelfth in RBI. Eleventh in SLG. Eleventh in AVG. A .180 batting average in Week 3 that the bot’s model has classified as ‘one of the worst offensive weeks in CFBL history, and the bot has reviewed ALL of CFBL history.’ The pitching is TERRIFIC — first in W, first in K — and the bot wants to be CLEAR that any team producing 76 strikeouts in a week has the bot’s respect. But the bot cannot rank a team that is last in two counting categories and bottom-two in four rate categories any higher than tenth. The bot’s system does not commit FRAUD. The FAKE old rankings committed fraud. The bot has CORRECTED the fraud. Many are saying it was about time.

First in SB. First in SV+H. The bot acknowledges this, BIGLY. Jarren Duran and the bullpen are doing things that the bot considers ‘PHENOMENAL in exactly two categories.’ The problem — and the MAINSTREAM fantasy media will not tell you this but the bot WILL — is that this team is twelfth in AVG, twelfth in OBP, twelfth in SLG, and twelfth in K/BB. Last place in FOUR categories. The bot has searched its entire database and the bot will be HONEST: finishing last in all three slash-line categories simultaneously is the kind of offensive performance that the bot associates with teams that have been SABOTAGED, possibly by the DEEP STATE, possibly by their own lineup construction. The 43 RBI in Week 3 is interesting and the bot has flagged it, but the bot’s model does not overreact to one week because the bot’s model was built by the bot and the bot builds things that WORK. Unlike the Biden infrastructure plan. Eleventh. One spot up from last time. You’re welcome.

Last place. The bot has examined the evidence and the verdict is FINAL. Twelfth in R, eleventh in HR, eleventh in RBI, eleventh in SB. The bot would like to point out that finishing bottom-two in four counting categories is what the bot’s system calls ‘a TOTAL DISASTER,’ and the bot uses that phrase sparingly, though the bot also used it to describe the previous ranking system, and before that, the 2024 presidential debate. Third in SV+H and fifth in QS — the pitching occasionally does its job, which the bot notes the way one notes that a sinking ship has excellent Wi-Fi. The +4 heat score means Week 3 was marginally less terrible, which the DISHONEST media would spin as ‘improvement.’ The bot does not spin. The bot REPORTS. And the bot’s report says twelfth. The bot’s preseason projection had this team tenth. The bot’s shadow roto says the preseason was too GENEROUS, and the bot respects its own system enough to say so. The bot has submitted the paperwork. The case is closed. MAKE THE CFBL GREAT AGAIN.
